rationale: The citing paper references Shen, Zhang, and Krishna (2016) at a specific methodological point, not just in a general literature list. In Experiment 2b, it explicitly uses/adapts Shen et al.'s mental imagery items (e.g., imagined enjoying/drinking the product) to operationalize an alternative mechanism. That borrowed measure is then analyzed to test whether mental imagery mediates the slow-motion effect, so the citation plays a concrete role in the study design and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies measurement items from Shen et al. (2016) to capture mental imagery in its experiments. It uses this borrowed operationalization to test and rule out mental imagery as an alternative process behind its focal effect.
